description:
You are acting as a build-and-release engineer who needs to keep a “latest” symbolic link up to date so that other automation tools always pick up the newest compiled build.

Current workspace
1. Directory         : /home/user/builds
2. Existing items in that directory:
   • build_v1.0.0/            (ordinary directory, already populated)  
   • build_v1.1.0/            (ordinary directory, already populated)  
   • build_v1.2.0/            (ordinary directory, already populated)  
   • latest                   (symbolic link that still points to build_v1.1.0)

Task to perform
Update /home/user/builds/latest so that it becomes a symbolic link to /home/user/builds/build_v1.2.0 instead of the older build_v1.1.0 directory.

Important verification rules (follow exactly—these will be tested automatically)
A. After you finish, `/home/user/builds/latest` must:
   1. Exist.
   2. Be a symbolic link (not a regular directory or file).
   3. Resolve **precisely** to this absolute path:  
      /home/user/builds/build_v1.2.0

B. The names and timestamps of the three build directories themselves must remain unchanged.

C. No extra files or directories should be added inside /home/user/builds.

Only one terminal command is needed to complete the job. No additional log files, text files, or console prints are required—just ensure the symbolic link points to the correct target when you are done.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts
/home/user/builds/                           (directory)
/home/user/builds/build_v1.0.0/              (directory)
/home/user/builds/build_v1.1.0/              (directory)
/home/user/builds/build_v1.2.0/              (directory)
/home/user/builds/latest  -> build_v1.1.0    (symbolic link pointing to /home/user/builds/build_v1.1.0)

Expected final filesystem state after correct completion
/home/user/builds/build_v1.0.0/              (unchanged directory)
/home/user/builds/build_v1.1.0/              (unchanged directory)
/home/user/builds/build_v1.2.0/              (unchanged directory)
/home/user/builds/latest  -> build_v1.2.0    (symbolic link now pointing to /home/user/builds/build_v1.2.0)

The test harness will:
1. Evaluate that /home/user/builds/latest exists.
2. Confirm it is a symlink.
3. Readlink(1) the symlink and expect the exact string “/home/user/builds/build_v1.2.0”.
No other side-effects are checked.
